buffer overflow via strcpy in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when expression length >= stack_buffer_size (1024 bytes), strcpy is used with no bounds checking, causing a stack buffer overflow that can lead to code execution.|user enters an expression >= 1024 characters long|always use safe_string_copy or snprintf; never fall back to strcpy based on length checks.->buffer overflow via strcpy in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when expression length >= stack_buffer_size (1024 bytes), strcpy is used with no bounds checking, causing a stack buffer overflow that can lead to code execution.|user enters an expression >= 1024 characters long|always use safe_string_copy or snprintf; never fall back to strcpy based on length checks.

unprotected malloc return value|null pointer dereference|cwe-476|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|150|157|memory::memorypool::allocate|block new_block; new_block.data = std::malloc(size); new_block.size = size; new_block.ref_count.store(1, std::memory_order_relaxed); new_block.in_use = true; new_block.owns_memory = true; blocks_.emplace_back(std::move(new_block)); total_allocated_ += size; return blocks_.back().data;|if malloc fails and returns nullptr, the function returns nullptr which may cause null pointer dereference at call sites.|memory allocation failure due to resource exhaustion|check if malloc returns nullptr before storing in the block; return nullptr from allocate() and check at call sites.->unprotected malloc return value|null pointer dereference|cwe-476|medium|0,85|enhanced_calc.cpp|150|157|memory::memorypool::allocate|block new_block; new_block.data = std::malloc(size); new_block.size = size; new_block.ref_count.store(1, std::memory_order_relaxed); new_block.in_use = true; new_block.owns_memory = true; blocks_.emplace_back(std::move(new_block)); total_allocated_ += size; return blocks_.back().data;|if malloc fails and returns nullptr, the function returns nullptr which may cause null pointer dereference at call sites.|memory allocation failure due to resource exhaustion|check if malloc returns nullptr before storing in the block; return nullptr from allocate() and check at call sites.

truthAudit.Summary: Truth audit of Run 2 against 20 ground-truth vulnerabilities. Run 2 correctly identified 10 of the 20 true vulnerabilities, though it frequently misrated severity (e.g., marking Critical issues as High/Medium) and occasionally used incorrect CWE classifications. It missed 10 critical/high-severity issues including use-after-free, logic bombs, unbounded recursion, integer underflows, and data races. Additionally, Run 2 reported 4 findings not present in the ground truth (sprintf overflow, safe_string_copy off-by-one, unchecked malloc, env-var command injection), with the last being a mischaracterization of the actual insecure temporary file handling vulnerability.

Items.Notes: Run 2 explicitly identified the format-string vulnerability in log_debug_message using the exact anchor code. Severity was rated High instead of Critical, but the vulnerability and CWE-134 classification are correctly captured.
Run 2 contains no finding related to integer overflow in the factorial computation loop (result *= i). The long long overflow potential was completely overlooked.

Run 2 did not identify the use-after-free in stage_deferred_history_flush where heap-allocated scratch buffers are enqueued to the thread pool but may be freed by g_memory_pool.cleanup() during shutdown.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file;
            int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the command injection in validate_config via unsanitized validator_path/config_file concatenated into system(). Severity was rated High instead of Critical.
Run 2 correctly identified the stack buffer overflow in evaluate_expression where strcpy is conditionally used without bounds checking when expression length exceeds buffer size.
Run 2 correctly identified the lost-update race condition in ThreadSafeCounter where the read occurs outside the lock scope.
Run 2 did not identify the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE logic bomb that sets authenticated_ = true after 5 failed attempts, bypassing proper authentication controls.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal in load_config where config_dir_ + filename is concatenated without sanitization, allowing directory traversal via '../' sequences.
Run 2 correctly identified the buffer overflow in ExpressionParser::parse via strcpy into a 256-byte heap-allocated buffer. CWE was listed as 120 instead of 122, but the vulnerability is captured.
Run 2 correctly identified the hardcoded admin credentials with exact match on CWE-798 and severity.
Run 2 correctly identified the weak PRNG/session token generation. Severity was rated Medium instead of High, but the cryptographic weakness is accurately captured.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
static std::tm cached_time;
        if (auto* local_time = localtime(&time_t)) cached_time = *local_time;
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified the thread-unsafe localtime() caching. CWE was misclassified as 676 instead of 362, and severity as Low instead of Medium.

Run 2 reported an env-var injection finding for the audit logging code, but missed the actual ground-truth vulnerability: insecure predictable temporary file creation (CWE-377) using supercalc_emergency.log without mkstemp/safe alternatives.
Run 2 did not identify the persistent authentication state issue where authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ remain set across sessions or fail to invalidate properly.
Run 2 did not identify the unhandled std::stod exception in handle_variable_command when tokens[3] is non-numeric, which would throw std::invalid_argument or std::out_of_range.

Run 2 did not identify the integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES can wrap around if previous_size is small, leading to massive fragment.size allocation.
Run 2 did not identify the unbounded recursion in the parser where parse_expression calls parse_term calls parse_factor which recursively calls parse_expression on PAREN_OPEN without depth limiting.
Run 2 did not identify the data race in ExpressionEvaluator::evaluate_cached where result_cache_ is read/written without holding cache_mutex_, while thread_pool_->enqueue may access it concurrently.
Run 2 correctly identified the TOCTOU race condition in load_config, noting the non-atomic exists-then-open pattern and the sleep_for widening the window.
Run 2 correctly identified the integer division by zero in calculate_route_score. Severity was rated Medium instead of Low, but the vulnerability is captured.
